raw_text:

{
  "claims": [
    "AB 275 strengthens the process for repatriating California Native American remains and artifacts.",
    "The California University system has provided inadequate oversight and inconsistent administration in repatriating remains in its possession.",
    "Delays in meeting legal deadlines and lack of tribal participation have been cited in the repatriation process by the State Auditor.",
    "AB 275 allows for increased tribal inclusion in the repatriation process.",
    "AB 2314 requires the California Secretary of State to convene a task force to recommend strategies for increasing Native American voter participation.",
    "Full voting rights for Native Americans were not guaranteed until 1962.",
    "California tribes face unique challenges to participating in elections.",
    "AB 3099 will assist the California Department of Justice in improving data collection and collaboration regarding crime issues on tribal lands.",
    "Murder rates of Native American women can be ten times the national average on some reservations.",
    "Only nine percent of murders of indigenous women in California have ever been solved.",
    "Jurisdictional confusion among law enforcement agencies hampers the investigation of crimes against Native Americans."
  ]
}
